title: Inquires about building a menu for cross-workspace boards
excerpt:
Our company has a few workspaces with boards that users need to access. Navigating from one workspace to another is cumbersome. 
Can we provide them with a list of all boards they can access in one place? Like building a…

posts.author: TheKribus (Michael Kribus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Our company has a few workspaces with boards that users need to access. Navigating from one workspace to another is cumbersome.

Can we provide them with a list of all boards they can access in one place? Like building a menu that gives them access to cross-workspace boards?

Putting all boards in one workspace is not an option at this point.

Thanks

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Michael, you can favorite boards (click the star), and use the favorites tab (recently improved).

image324×184 9.9 KB

image317×372 19.1 KB

image783×121 11.1 KB

posts.author: TheKribus (Michael Kribus)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is not suutable for our organization, we have +100 users
We cant go one by one anf add more then 30+ items

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@TheKribus

You could create a board where each item is a board with a link to that board. This could give everyone a reminder and link to each of the boards they have access to.

Jim - The Monday Man
Get Custom Apps, Integrations & Automations for monday

posts.author: lpenndorf (Lori)
posts.cookedHtml: You could create a Document in Monday and make it sort of your “hub” page and have links to all the boards needed by a certain team. then you could pretty it up with titles and other verbiage.

posts.author: TheKribus (Michael Kribus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Indeed, I have considered option, but I prefer to use a document with links if that is the direction.
However, this is also an issue because clicking on links inside a document opens a new browser tab.
